title: Novena to St. Rita of Cascia
description: A novena to Saint Rita of Cascia with three thanksgiving days after the first nine days of prayer.

days.title: Day One
days.body:
In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit.

O Saint Rita, worker of miracles, look upon me with your merciful eyes, I who suffer and shed tears. Behold my bleeding heart, surrounded by thorns. Look upon my eyes, O Saint Rita, for they can shed tears no more, since so many tears have already fallen from them!

I am weary and discouraged. I am weary even of praying. Shall I lose hope amid these trials of my life? No! O Saint Rita, come quickly to my aid. Do not Christians call you the Saint of impossible cases, and do they not proclaim your greatness to those who have fallen into despair? Obtain for me, then, from God all that I ask of you: *(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).*

Everyone praises your holiness. Everyone tells of your wonders and miracles. Everyone speaks of what is done through you. Shall I alone be rejected and left discouraged because you have not heard me? No. Intercede for me before the Lord Jesus, that He may have mercy on me and help me in my troubles and anxieties. Through your intercession, O Saint Rita, may I receive that which my heart so deeply desires.

Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. You help the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Two
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O wonder-working Saint Rita of Cascia, I see that the path of your life was filled with many trials and piercing thorns, which I fear tore your heart into pieces. O Rita, true martyr of bitter sorrow, you who tasted, drop by drop, until you had drained the whole chalice of deep suffering: I think upon your heart, utterly worn out. I turn to you and ask God through your intercession: *(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).*

You know the meaning of a bleeding heart. You also know the martyrdom of the soul. You were wounded greatly and suffered deeply. Through those sufferings, I beg you to help me in my anxieties. Is it not true that in this difficult time our Lord Jesus has prepared for us a way of obtaining grace, through the miracles which human beings may ask of you with hope and, through your intercession, may obtain?

O beloved Saint, look upon this crushed soul, weeping and sorrowing before you. I place my trust in you, O Saint Rita. Intercede for me before Jesus in heaven, that I may receive all that I ask of God through your powerful intercession.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Three
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

I look here and there without finding any place of rest, while my eyes are overshadowed by sorrow. No hope of this world brings joy to my heart, which is weighed down with grief; I feel utterly lost. But you, beloved Saint Rita, who shine like a star giving incomparable light among the powers and pillars of the Catholic Church, I know that you illumine my dark path and give courage to my heart that mourns in sorrow. I believe in you, and with longing I await the granting of what I ask: ***(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).***

Present my petition to Jesus of the Cross. I remember that difficult time for you, when your heart passed through trials, when in obedience you accepted marriage to a man who tormented you and even threatened your life. Yet you loved him greatly, and when he died you mourned his death with deep sorrow. By the heroic sacrifice which you offered to God in your sons, praying that they might not be defiled by sin, intercede for me before God. This is the martyrdom of your heart. Through the suffering you chose, namely to live your life within the convent; through the sufferings which the Lord Jesus Christ permitted to come upon you so that you might be purified in your sorrows; through the many trials that befell you and the many sacrifices you offered: intercede for me, that I may be granted the petition for which I wait with longing.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Four
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O Saint of impossible cases, and advocate of those who have lost hope: these are pure titles, full of hope for souls tried by sorrow. To you, Saint Rita of Cascia, who, when we find ourselves alone in our lives, surrounded by sorrow and death, come to us like a gardener from heaven, bringing into our dry hearts hope and living water. O hope of my heart, which has dried up because of the heavy burden of sorrow: the weight of misfortune has overwhelmed me, doubts frighten me, and I do not know where to turn to seek the help I need. To you, good Rita, in whom I greatly trust and on whom I rely, I come with confidence that you will hear me. Through the power of your intercession before the throne of God, I hope to be heard in my petition: ***(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).***

Show yourself once more to be powerful and merciful. Prove to me also that it is true, as your followers confess, that you are the Saint of impossible cases and the advocate of those who have lost hope.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Five
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O beloved spouse of Jesus, Saint Rita of Cascia, I come to you once again. I am at your feet. I am drawn by your goodness toward souls that are afflicted and troubled. You see how my heart has been crushed by the hardships of this world. I no longer find a precious smile; my soul has been invaded by evil doubts, my faith has been shaken, and the shock of despair, like the shock of death, yawns at my feet. Guide me, O beloved Saint; what shall I do in these dreadful circumstances? Advise me: to whom shall I go in this dangerous state of my life? If truly you are the Saint of all desperate situations, come to my aid, for I feel lost. I come to you, who always have compassion on those who suffer. I come to you: help me. Will you not have mercy on me because of my condition? Saint Rita, I know that you are very good. I know this, and for that reason I place myself wholly in your hands, with all my heart, so that through Jesus you may answer the petition which, with great faith, I make: ***(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).***

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Six
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

I know that because of my sins, my prayers have been covered by a cloud and cannot reach your throne. My sins are the cause of my great punishments. I do not have the courage to raise my voice to Jesus, because many times I failed to value His mercy. And now I see His great and just power. His righteous hand is turned toward me. I recognize that I do not deserve the blessings of His forgiveness, for I am overwhelmed by great sorrow. Without hope, I weep over my sins. Tell me, Saint Rita: shall I continue to despair? No. Our Lord Jesus Christ has chosen you as His powerful advocate, so that you may intercede for me before my Jesus on high, obtaining forgiveness for my grave faults and a pure intention never to offend Him again. With this firm resolve of my lips and my heart, I turn to you. O wonder-working Saint, intercede for me before the Lord Jesus for the virtues and graces which I so greatly need: ***(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).***

In this time of great weariness, sorrow, and loneliness, O Rita, your word to Jesus will open His mercy and forgiveness. Speak one word, and Jesus will hear you.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Seven
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O beloved Rita, whose life was one of sorrow, turn your merciful face toward me, who suffer bitterly. I ask that my prayers, my weariness, and my stumbling may reach your gentle heart, so that it may be opened to me. May your mercy, which you always showed to your neighbour, your words of peace and consolation, and the holy counsel you gave to all people, draw me toward you, who dwell in heaven and are the intercessor of all who have lost hope. Before your great miracles, death turns back, the blind receive light, the lame stand, the paralyzed are healed, and every infirmity of soul and body is completely removed. Shall I alone be left without consolation through your intercession? I will not believe it, nor can I believe it. I, too, firmly believe in your protection and in your intercession before the throne of God. I have great hope that I shall obtain peace, that my heart shall be calm, and that the graces I ask for shall be granted to me: ***(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).***

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Eight
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O holy image of the glorious Cross, when I behold you kneeling before the sacred feet of the Lord, when I contemplate your forehead wounded by a thorn taken from the crown of thorns of Jesus Christ, your beloved Spouse, my faith in you blossoms in a wondrous way. Truly, you are the chosen servant of God. God has bestowed upon you the mark of His kingdom; He has sanctified you and made you wholly His own. Therefore, I was not mistaken when I placed all my trust in you, and when I placed myself completely under your most merciful protection. And now is the favourable time for you to help me, knowing the needs and helps which I ask from you. I believe that this prayer to you, most beloved mother, will not be in vain. You, wonder-worker, benefactress of heavenly things, hope of all humanity: I pray and beseech you, by the merits of your love for Jesus Christ our Saviour, intercede for me, that I may be granted these things which I ask: ***(here make the need you desire to ask of God through Saint Rita).***

God refuses you nothing; through your intercession, miracles are performed, and special graces and favours are obtained. Therefore, with all my heart and with sincere hope, I wait, trusting that I shall receive from the Lord God these things for which I have prayed.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Nine
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

I come before you, O Saint Rita, on this final day of this holy Novena. I feel the beating of my heart in this difficult period of my troubled life. I believe that my petition will be fulfilled by the Lord through your holy intercession. But if it is not possible, I raise my voice, pleading earnestly for your mercy and consolation. Do not let me depart without receiving your comfort.

O Saint Rita, I beg you to console me by the merits of the sufferings you endured here on earth and in your solitude of prayer. Console me by the merits of the generosity you showed toward those in need. Hear me by the merits of your great love for our Lord Jesus Christ and for His Mother, the holy and Immaculate Virgin Mary. By the merits of that holy thorn which our Lord Jesus Christ granted to you, help me and favour me. This is my final cry to you, holy mother, Saint of impossible cases and intercessor of those who have lost hope. I ask you to reveal yourself in your true light, so that God may grant me these things for which I pray. With your help, I resolve to renounce my wrongful way of life, so that I may become a sharer in the power of God and in the beauty of the things of heaven. I also promise to make pilgrimage to your place of solitude, O Saint of prayer, there in Cascia, and/or to your statue, so that I may unite myself with you in adoration and thanksgiving for the heavenly favours I have received through your intercession from our beloved Saviour, Jesus Christ.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of the Thorn of St. Rita, Psalm 51, or Psalm 130.*

days.title: Day Ten
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

Eternal Father, whose mercy has no end and whose goodness is everlasting, look upon me as I bow humbly before Your glorious throne. From the depths of my heart, I raise a hymn of thanksgiving for the love You have granted me through Your servant, Saint Rita of Cascia. I recognize, O my God, that I do not deserve the favours You have bestowed upon me; yet, disregarding my shortcomings, You have listened to my prayer through the intercession of Saint Rita of Cascia.

O Saint Rita, renowned wonder-worker of the Catholic Church, to you I offer my heartfelt thanks. My mouth has not enough words to express what I feel within my heart. O beloved Saint, it is true that all who trust in your intercession are not disappointed. Fill me always with the grace which I may obtain through the mercy of God, and grant me your protection in life and in death.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. *You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.*

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of St. Rita.*

days.title: Day Eleven
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O loving Heart of Jesus, ever rich and generous in mercy without end, You who told the prophet that You take no pleasure in the death of the wicked, but rather that he should turn and live: look upon me in my lowliness. I thank You with all my heart for the special graces You have granted me through the merits of Your chosen servant, Saint Rita of Cascia. What shall I return to You, O Lord, for the love You have bestowed upon me? O my God, I will never cease to thank You, You who have always been good even to all who despise You.

And you, beloved Saint Rita, how shall I repay the favours you have obtained for me? My heart is filled with incomparable joy. I will thank you without end. O good Saint, you who are close to our Lord, carry my thanks to beloved Jesus. Tell Him that my heart is filled with gratitude to Him and to you, and that I will always hate sin, so that I may continue to enjoy His mercy and His heavenly guidance.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. *You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.*

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of St. Rita.*

days.title: Day Twelve
days.body:
**In the Name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.**

O my beloved God, who do not allow those who hope in You and believe in You to be overcome by suffering, but who lovingly hear their prayers: from the depths of my heart I offer You my everlasting thanks for the favours You have granted me through the intercession of Your chosen servant, Saint Rita. Receive, O Lord Jesus, my sincere thanksgiving, rising like the sweet fragrance of sacrifice. Receive it through the hands of Saint Rita. O beloved Jesus, help me always to be watchful in receiving Your generosity, Your goodness, and Your mercy.

And you, O wonder-working Saint, whose name is glorified with great joy throughout the whole world, and whose intercession brings miracles: receive my thanks from the depths of my heart. I would desire that everyone should know you, and that all people should love you, for then all would recognize your heavenly power. O bright star of the Order of Saint Augustine, in thanksgiving for the good things I have received through your many favours, I will spread your devotion among all people, telling them that you are truly the Saint of impossible cases, the advocate of those who have lost hope, and the intercessor of all who have lost the hopes of this world.

**Our Father (3), Hail Mary (3), Glory Be (3)**

Through Saint Rita: may fevers, sores and plague, the germs of many diseases, demons, and wrath disappear. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

Through your intercession: thunder, earthquakes, and fire have no power. By your holiness, every snare, every great danger, and every threat disappears. *You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.*

Spain proclaims you the Saint of impossible cases. Many people sing your praises. Cascia glorifies your name. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

To God the Holy One, and to His Son, be praise, glory, and honour; praise throughout all ages of everlasting love. **You help the blind, the deaf, the mute, and the lame; children earnestly implore you, and the dead are restored to life.**

**V: You have marked, O Lord, Your servant Rita.**
**R: With the seal of love and suffering.**

**LET US PRAY:** O God, who graciously adorned Saint Rita with grace, so that she loved her enemies and bore in her soul and upon her forehead the marks of love and suffering: we ask You, through her intercession, for the grace to forgive our enemies and to meditate upon the sorrows of Your Passion, so that we may receive the reward You have promised to the meek and to those who suffer. You who live and reign, forever and ever. Amen.

*After completing this section, pray the Litany of St. Rita, the Litany of St. Rita.*

closing_prayer.title: Litany of St. Rita
closing_prayer.description: A responsive litany to St. Rita, intercessor in difficult and impossible cases.
closing_prayer.body:
LITANY OF ST. RITA OF CASCIA

Lord, have mercy,

Christ, have mercy,

Lord, have mercy,

Response: Have mercy on us

God the Father of Heaven,

God the Son, Redeemer of the world,

God the Holy Spirit,

Holy Trinity, one God,

Response: Pray for us

Mary Immaculate, Mother of God

Mary, Mother and comforter of those who suffer

Mary, Queen of all Saints

Mary, beloved intercessor of St. Rita

Response: Pray for us

St. Rita, gift from Heaven:

St. Rita, our powerful intercessor:

St. Rita, endowed with glory:

St. Rita, admired from your childhood:

St. Rita, lover of solitude:

St. Rita, model of purity of heart:

St. Rita, model of all gentleness:

St. Rita, model of a good wife:

St. Rita, excellent model of a devoted mother:

St. Rita, model of true forgiveness:

St. Rita, living model of self-denial:

St. Rita, mirror of religious life:

St. Rita, mirror of obedience:

St. Rita, admirable in patience:

St. Rita, hero of self-sacrifice:

St. Rita, witness in doing penance:

St. Rita, holy widow:

St. Rita, generous to the poor:

St. Rita, generous to the poor:

St. Rita, zealous in answering the holy call:

St. Rita, miraculously admitted into the convent:

St. Rita, fragrance of myrrh:

St. Rita, garden of every virtue:

St. Rita, beloved of Jesus Crucified:

St. Rita, wounded by a thorn on your forehead:

St. Rita, beloved daughter of the Virgin Mary:

St. Rita, thirsty for Divine Love:

St. Rita, clothed with glory:

St. Rita, pearl of Heaven:

St. Rita, glory of the Order of St. Augustine:

St. Rita, precious treasure of Umbria:

St. Rita, powerful in holiness:

St. Rita, relief of those in distress:

St. Rita, comforter of those who suffer:

St. Rita, anchor of salvation:

St. Rita, intercessor for the sick:

St. Rita, intercessor in impossible cases:

St. Rita, refuge of those in danger:

St. Rita, powerful help for all:

St. Rita, wonder of the world:

St. Rita, received joyfully into Heaven:

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, spare us, O Lord

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, graciously hear us, O Lord

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us

V: You have marked, O Lord, your servant Rita

R: With the seal of love and suffering

LET US PRAY:

O God, who in your unfailing goodness have been pleased to hear the intercession of your servant Rita, grant us through her intercession those things that are impossible by human strength. Have mercy on us who suffer, and help all who do not believe in you to know that you are the reward of the humble, the comforter of all who are afraid, and the strength of the weak. We ask this through Christ our Lord. Amen

thanksgiving_section.title: Thanksgiving (Part Two)
thanksgiving_section.description: Three thanksgiving days after the nine days of the novena, before continuing with Day Ten through Day Twelve.
thanksgiving_section.body:
*Part Two*

*Three Days of Thanksgiving to God in Honour of St. Rita of Cascia*

After praying the nine (9) days of this Novena to Saint Rita of Cascia, you are invited to pray three (3) days of thanksgiving to God in honour of Saint Rita.

This thanksgiving is offered for the answering of the prayers you prayed during the 9 days. It is also to thank God for allowing you to complete the novena safely, even if you think your petition has not been answered. It is our duty to thank God at all times and for everything (1 Thess. 5:18).

Each day, after completing the prayers of this thanksgiving section, you are also required to pray **the Litany of Saint Rita of Cascia** or **Psalm 51 for Asking God’s Mercy.** You may choose one of these two prayers: the Litany of Saint Rita or Psalm 51.
